_schema: Naturalistic meeting scripts for realistic ASR + demo audio (SEPARATE from test-transcripts.json, which holds the agent gold labels). Rendered by scripts/gen-meeting-audio.ts with ElevenLabs v3. Per turn: `say`=text sent to TTS, WITH audio tags ([sighs],[laughs],[overlapping],[strong French accent]), ellipses for pauses, CAPS for emphasis, and code-switching; `text`=the clean gold transcript a human would write for WER — the STANDARD-SPELLING intended words (accent normalized to canonical spelling, tags/CAPS/ellipses stripped), NOT a phonetic transcription; `kind`=talk|offtopic|crosstalk (offtopic should be skipped by the router; crosstalk overlaps the previous turn); `overlapMs`=for crosstalk, how many ms before the previous clip ends this one starts; `lang`=ISO-639-1 override for this turn. The point: people are having their normal meeting — nobody is prompting the agent. The buildable intent is buried in messy talk (see `note`); accents are carried by the audio tag, not by misspelling real words.

scenarios.title: Daily Standup (that goes sideways)
scenarios.setting: Three engineers on a casual morning standup. Nobody is talking to a tool — they're just venting about their week. A board they clearly need surfaces mid-rant.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.say: Okay, okay... are we all here? Morning. [yawns] How was everyone's weekend?
turns.text: Okay, okay, are we all here? Morning. How was everyone's weekend?

turns.speaker: Sam
turns.say: [laughs] Don't. I spent like six hours building a desk and the last screw just... did not exist. It was not in the bag.
turns.text: Don't. I spent like six hours building a desk and the last screw just, did not exist. It was not in the bag.

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: Mine was good, went climbing. Uh, can we— I've got a hard stop at half past, so.
turns.text: Mine was good, went climbing. Uh, can we, I've got a hard stop at half past, so.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.say: Yeah, yeah, let's go. Sam, you start?
turns.text: Yeah, yeah, let's go. Sam, you start?

turns.speaker: Sam
turns.note: pain point: no visibility into who's blocked
turns.say: So I'm still stuck on the auth thing, waiting on Theo's review. And honestly... [sighs] I keep losing track of who's blocked on what. It's all just Slack threads and, like, my memory.
turns.text: So I'm still stuck on the auth thing, waiting on Theo's review. And honestly, I keep losing track of who's blocked on what. It's all just Slack threads and, like, my memory.

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: [overlapping] —yeah it's a total mess—
turns.text: yeah it's a total mess

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.say: Right?? Like I genuinely could not tell you what's in flight right now.
turns.text: Right? Like I genuinely could not tell you what's in flight right now.

turns.speaker: Sam
turns.note: buildable intent (kanban / blockers board) expressed casually, NOT as a request — softened per review so it reads as a gripe, not a spec
turns.say: Honestly I just want, like, ONE screen. Where I can just... drag the thing from, I dunno, doing to blocked when it actually moves. Just shove the cards around.
turns.text: Honestly I just want, like, one screen. Where I can just drag the thing from, I dunno, doing to blocked when it actually moves. Just shove the cards around.

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: Yeah and the blocked stuff should just, like, be screaming red at me. So I can't ignore it. [laughs]
turns.text: Yeah and the blocked stuff should just, like, be screaming red at me. So I can't ignore it.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.say: [laughs] Okay, that's— yeah. Anyway... did someone kill the last of the oat milk? Be honest.
turns.text: Okay, that's, yeah. Anyway, did someone kill the last of the oat milk? Be honest.

turns.speaker: Sam
turns.say: Wasn't me. Okay I gotta jump — same time tomorrow?
turns.text: Wasn't me. Okay I gotta jump, same time tomorrow?

scenarios.title: Growth Sync (Brazilian team)
scenarios.setting: A Brazilian growth team, mostly Portuguese with English product jargon code-switched in (churn, MRR). They want a panel but say it offhand, between a coffee spill and last night's match.

turns.speaker: Bia
turns.say: Bom dia, gente. Calma que o Rafa ainda tá entrando... Rafa, cê tá aí?
turns.text: Bom dia, gente. Calma que o Rafa ainda tá entrando, Rafa, cê tá aí?

turns.speaker: Rafa
turns.say: [laughs] Tô, tô, desculpa — o café derramou na mesa inteira agora.
turns.text: Tô, tô, desculpa, o café derramou na mesa inteira agora.

turns.speaker: Bia
turns.say: [laughs] Clássico. Bom... growth review. Rafa, como é que tá o churn esse mês?
turns.text: Clássico. Bom, growth review. Rafa, como é que tá o churn esse mês?

turns.speaker: Rafa
turns.say: Então, o churn subiu pra quatro e pouco por cento. O MRR tá em cento e quarenta mil, crescendo, mas esse churn aí tá me preocupando.
turns.text: Então, o churn subiu pra quatro e pouco por cento. O MRR tá em cento e quarenta mil, crescendo, mas esse churn aí tá me preocupando.

turns.speaker: Lu
turns.note: pain point ('cinco planilha' is intentional casual BR concord, per review — keep)
turns.say: Eu queria conseguir ver isso sem ter que abrir cinco planilha. Tipo... [sighs] toda semana é a mesma caça.
turns.text: Eu queria conseguir ver isso sem ter que abrir cinco planilha. Tipo, toda semana é a mesma caça.

turns.speaker: Bia
turns.note: buildable intent (dashboard) — softened per review: WAU implied ('quem realmente tá usando'), not named
turns.say: Pois é. Sério, eu só queria um painel com tudo isso numa tela só — o churn, o MRR, e quem realmente tá usando.
turns.text: Pois é. Sério, eu só queria um painel com tudo isso numa tela só, o churn, o MRR, e quem realmente tá usando.

turns.speaker: Rafa
turns.say: [overlapping] —isso, tudo num lugar só—
turns.text: isso, tudo num lugar só

turns.speaker: Lu
turns.say: E que atualize sozinho, né? Não dá pra ficar exportando CSV na mão toda semana.
turns.text: E que atualize sozinho, né? Não dá pra ficar exportando CSV na mão toda semana.

turns.speaker: Bia
turns.say: Exato. Ah... vocês viram o jogo ontem?
turns.text: Exato. Ah, vocês viram o jogo ontem?

turns.speaker: Rafa
turns.say: Nem me fala. Fui dormir PUTO da vida. [laughs]
turns.text: Nem me fala. Fui dormir puto da vida.

turns.speaker: Bia
turns.say: [laughs] Tá, foco, foco. Lu, cê fecha o resto depois?
turns.text: Tá, foco, foco. Lu, cê fecha o resto depois?

scenarios.title: Launch Room (people talking over each other)
scenarios.setting: A launch jam with a French teammate and a lot of cross-talk. The landing-page they need comes out as a half-finished mumble, not a spec.

turns.speaker: Nora
turns.say: Alright, launch is in — what, two weeks? [exhales] We need the landing page to actually convert this time.
turns.text: Alright, launch is in, what, two weeks? We need the landing page to actually convert this time.

turns.speaker: Emile
turns.note: accent carried by the tag, not by misspelling real words (per review — avoids v3 saying the verb 'sings')
turns.say: [strong French accent] Yes, and last time it was, how you say, too busy. Too many things on the page.
turns.text: Yes, and last time it was, how you say, too busy. Too many things on the page.

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: [overlapping] —WAY too busy—
turns.text: way too busy

turns.speaker: Ava
turns.say: So strip it then. Like one thing, one big promise, and just put the email box right there. Done.
turns.text: So strip it then. Like one thing, one big promise, and just put the email box right there. Done.

turns.speaker: Nora
turns.note: buildable intent (dark landing page: hero + signup + cards) — softened: exact card count dropped to 'a few'
turns.say: Yeah... honestly I just picture, like, a HUGE headline, the signup right under it, and a few little cards below. Dark. Punchy.
turns.text: Yeah, honestly I just picture, like, a huge headline, the signup right under it, and a few little cards below. Dark. Punchy.

turns.speaker: Emile
turns.say: [strong French accent] And the hero image from the deck, no? The one with... you know the one I mean.
turns.text: And the hero image from the deck, no? The one with, you know the one I mean.

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: Wait, are we actually doing dark mode though? My EYES, man. [laughs]
turns.text: Wait, are we actually doing dark mode though? My eyes, man.

turns.speaker: Ava
turns.say: [overlapping] —it's a launch, of course it's dark—
turns.text: it's a launch, of course it's dark

turns.speaker: Nora
turns.say: Okay. Also — did the coffee machine die again or is that just me?
turns.text: Okay. Also, did the coffee machine die again or is that just me?

turns.speaker: Theo
turns.say: It's dead. Tragedy. ANYWAY — five-word headline, who's owning that?
turns.text: It's dead. Tragedy. Anyway, five-word headline, who's owning that?

turns.speaker: Ava
turns.say: I'll take it.
turns.text: I'll take it.
